channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Residents flee border area as Afghan and Pakistani forces clash over crossing closure
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — Residents have fled a border area between Afghanistan and Pakistan after forces from both sides clashed into the early hours of Tuesday, a Pakistani official said.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Arab leaders gather to endorse counterproposal to Trump's Gaza plan, with ceasefire's fate uncertain
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Arab leaders meeting in Cairo on Tuesday are set to endorse a counterproposal to U.S. President Donald Trump’s call for the Gaza Strip to be depopulated and transformed into a beach destination, even as the continuation of the Israel-Hamas ceasefire is uncertain.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: GOP senators urge Trump to salvage negotiations with Zelensky despite 'disappointing' blowup
item.description: Republican lawmakers are urging President Trump to salvage negotiations with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky in hopes of reaching a peace deal that halts Russian encroachment and possibly gives the United States access to Ukraine’s rich mineral wealth.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: Philippine fighter jet carrying 2 pilots goes missing during mission against insurgents in southern province
item.description: A Philippine air force fighter jet went missing during a night combat assault in support of ground forces who were battling insurgents in a southern province.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukrainians grapple with consequences of Trump's pause on military aid
item.description: Ukrainians are grappling with the consequences of the Trump administration’s decision to pause military aid vital for their fight against Russia’s invasion.
item.dc:creator: Samya Kullab, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pope Francis no longer requires mechanical ventilation after respiratory crisis
item.description: The Vatican says that Pope Francis has stabilized enough after two respiratory crises to resume using a nasal tube for oxygen, rather than a ventilation mask.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Some goods in the crosshairs of Trump's tariffs on Mexico, Canada and China
item.description: President Donald Trump imposed 25% taxes on imports from Canada and Mexico on Tuesday and doubled to 20% his levies on Chinese products.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Syrian refugee family that Pope Francis brought to Rome prays for him as they build new life
item.description: ROME (AP) — Just before breaking the Ramadan fast on Sunday evening, Hasan Zaheda played basketball with his son in the tiny courtyard of the basement-level apartment on Rome’s outskirts where the refugee family is rebuilding their lives.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US tariffs on Canada and Mexico take effect, as China takes aim at US farm exports
item.description: President Donald Trump’s long-threatened tariffs against Canada and Mexico have gone into effect, kicking off a trade war with America's neighbors.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Paul Wiseman And Rob Gillies, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Colombian president aims to reduce drug violence and weaken rebels by paying farmers to switch crops
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — Colombian President Gustavo Petro announced a plan Monday to reduce coca planting in a northeastern region rattled by rebel attacks by paying farmers while they switch to legal crops.
item.dc:creator: MANUEL RUEDA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Costa Rica and Panama coordinate to move southbound migrants
item.description: PEÑAS BLANCAS, Costa Rica (AP) — Costa Rica and Panama are coordinating to expedite southbound migrant transit through their countries along the same route that carried hundreds of thousands north in recent years, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: BERNY ARAYA adn JAVIER CÓRDOBA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kim's sister threatens a response to a US carrier's deployment in South Korea
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The powerful sister of North Korean leader Kim Jong Un threatened Tuesday to launch high-profile provocations in response to the arrival of a U.S. aircraft carrier in South Korea and other U.S. military activities, which she slammed as “confrontation hysteria of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republican says he's nominating Trump for Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: Rep. Darrell Issa (R-Calif.) said on Monday he is nominating President Trump for the Nobel Peace Prize. “Today I will nominate @realDonaldTrump for the Nobel Peace Prize,” Issa wrote in a post on X. “No one deserves it more.” Issa did not specify in his post why he was nominating the president.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Tuberville: Zelensky 'not even in the game' on Ukraine war talks
item.description: Sen. Tommy Tuberville (R-Ala.), a staunch supporter of President Trump’s, said Russia and the United States will ultimately determine the fate of the war in Ukraine, saying the war-torn democracy’s president, Volodymyr Zelensky, is “not even in the game.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Without US help, Zelenskyy has few options except to repair his relationship with the White House
item.description: Ukraine’s president has few options after last week’s astonishing Oval Office row with U.S. President Donald Trump, who berated the wartime leader.
item.dc:creator: Samya Kullab, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A driver rams a car into crowd in Germany's Mannheim, leaving 2 dead and 11 injured
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A driver rammed a car into a crowd Monday in the southwestern German city of Mannheim, and authorities said two people were killed and 11 others injured, five of them severely.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Starmer and Macron step up to shape European security as Trump roils relations
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The leaders of Britain and France are spearheading a desperate diplomatic drive to shore up Europe’s security, bolster Ukraine’s defenses and ensure the Trump administration doesn’t seek a ceasefire on terms that reward Moscow for invading its neighbor.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gabon's military leader announces he will run in April presidential election
item.description: LIBREVILLE, Gabon (AP) — Gabon's military leader announced Monday that he would run as a candidate in the oil-rich central African nation’s presidential election set for April 12. Gen.
item.dc:creator: YVES LAURENT GOMA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Austria's new government takes office after a record 5-month wait
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — Austria’s new government took office on Monday, with Christian Stocker taking over as chancellor at the head of a previously untried three-party coalition after a record five-month wait for a new administration.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANIE LIECHTENSTEIN and GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis suffers new breathing crises, is back on noninvasive ventilation: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis suffered two new episodes of acute respiratory crises Monday and was put back on noninvasive mechanical ventilation, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis rested well after overcoming setback from respiratory crisis
item.description: Pope Francis rested well after overcoming a setback in his recovery caused by a respiratory crisis, as he remains in the hospital recovering from pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel is criticized after it bars Gaza aid to pressure Hamas to accept a new ceasefire proposal
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel faced sharp criticism as it stopped the entry of all food and other supplies into Gaza on Sunday and warned of “additional consequences” for Hamas if a fragile ceasefire isn't extended.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK's Starmer says Europe is at ‘crossroads in history’ as leaders agree to steps to Ukraine peace
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British Prime Minister Keir Starmer rallied his European counterparts Sunday to shore up their borders and throw their full weight behind Ukraine as he announced outlines of a plan to end Russia’s war.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY and EMMA BURROWS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Francis off mechanical ventilation entirely, signaling he's overcome risks from respiratory crisis
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in stable condition and didn’t need any mechanical ventilation Sunday, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel supports a US proposal to extend Gaza ceasefire's first phase. But Hamas wants Phase 2
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel’s government said early Sunday it supports a proposal to extend the first phase of the ceasefire in Gaza through Ramadan and Passover, though Hamas has insisted on negotiating the truce’s second phase instead.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: USAID cuts are already hitting countries around the world. Here are 20 projects that have closed
item.description: Some 10,000 contracts with the U.S. Agency for International Development were terminated on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, WILSON MCMAKIN and MONIKA PRONCZUK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russia revels in Oval Office spectacle after Zelenskyy spars with Trump, Vance
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's heated exchange with U.S. President Donald Trump has Russian leaders reveling in the spectacle, with one saying Kyiv got a "slap down.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: World leaders back Zelenskyy following Trump, Vance Oval Office spat
item.description: World leaders pledge support for Ukraine and emphasize Russia is the "aggressor" following the Oval Office spat in which the Trump administration accused President Volodymyr Zelenskyy of being "disrespectful.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rose Girone, oldest living Holocaust survivor, dies at 113
item.description: Rose Girone, the oldest living survivor of the Holocaust and advocate for sharing the stories of survivors, died on Monday in New York at age 113.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Violent protests erupt in Greece on anniversary of deadly train crash
item.description: A protest over the Greek government’s response to the nation’s largest ever train crash turned violent on Friday as Scores of youths hurled rocks and gasoline bombs at police.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Tate brothers, who face human trafficking charges in Romania, arrive in Florida
item.description: After the brothers arrived in Florida, state Attorney General James Uthmeier said in a social media post that his office would conduct a “preliminary inquiry” into them.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Golden toilet was stolen from English palace in 'audacious raid,' prosecutor says
item.description: It was not your typical smash-and-grab burglary and the booty was precious: a toilet worth more than its weight in gold.
item.dc:creator: Brian Melley, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former Denver developer shot dead at luxury Belize home in likely 'hit': Police
item.description: Boris Mannsfeld was found shot to death at The Villas at Cocoplum, a luxury residential community on the Caribbean country's southeastern coast, Belizean media outlets reported.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Factor, NewsNation
